TextENGNew: "Mooo-orning, Mi-chan!"

Rena waved as Mion came running over.
I called out to her too.

"Good morning, Rena! Kei-chan!
Good work yesterday, both of you!"
"You too, Mi-chan — thanks for handling the cleanup!"
I wanted to offer Mion some words of appreciation for her hard work.
Even for a small village festival, this was a village-wide effort. The scale was nothing to scoff at.
"That must have been quite a lot of work, the cleanup."

"It was, let me tell you… we were at it until around three in the morning?
Then I went home, took a bath, and… went to sleep at four-thirty."
Her tone was breezy enough that I almost let it slide — but then the meaning of "four-thirty" landed and I stared at her.

"F-four-thirty?!
That means you barely slept at all!"

If it were me… I would absolutely have declared a personal snow day.
I was being completely, deeply sincere about that.

"Ahahaha!
Kei-kun, you're making your 'if it were me I'd have stayed home' face!
Hau~"
Ugh… nnkk.
Rena had read my mind…

"Ahahahaha!
Kei-chan is just so easy to read!"

According to Mion, the adults had been going at it until dawn.
You could see their devotion to the festival.
We chatted and laughed as we reached the classroom door and I put my hand on it.
…Right at that moment — my internal crisis-management system gave a little ping.
I looked up immediately — and sure enough, there was a chalkboard eraser balanced on the door!

Mufufufu… how naive.
I snatched it and stepped triumphantly into the room.
changeReason: New translation correctly renders 甘い as 'naive' (underestimating the trap) rather than 'sweet,' changing the speaker's attitude.

TextENGNew: But in the next instant, the familiar view in front of me flipped in a single second to the ceiling.
I only realized I'd been tripped after I hit the floor, rear-first, with a healthy dose of pain.
changeReason: Original says 'mild pain' but JPN したたかな痛み means severe/solid pain; new translation corrects this to 'healthy dose of pain.'

TextENGNew: To make matters worse, the chalkboard eraser landed squarely on my face, releasing a cloud of white powder.
I brushed it off and pushed myself upright to see — an abacus sprinting away from under my feet at full speed.

"My, my, Keiichi-san.
Backflipping the moment you enter the room — what a dedicated entertainer you are!"

I hauled myself to my feet and stalked toward the culprit — step, step.
Flick. Flick. Snap!
My index finger struck the target with tremendous force.

"Fuwaaa-aaah!!
Keiichi-san flicked me on the foreheaaad!!"
"You came close, but not yet, I'm afraid."
changeReason: Original translation adds non-existent meaning ('not even the worst of it'); new translation better captures the JPN sense of 'it was a fierce trap, but you're still not there yet.'

TextENGNew: Rena writhed in agony at the spectacle; Mion looked exasperated.
changeReason: Original misreads 悶絶 as 'on the verge of fainting' and 呆れる as 'stunned'; new correctly translates as 'writhed in agony' and 'exasperated.'

TextENGNew:
Everything was… exactly as it always was.
No classmates missing; the atmosphere unchanged.
The low murmur of unease that had been hovering at the back of my mind since last night dispersed all at once.
I looked around the classroom with a relieved expression.

A smile might have spread across my face without my realizing it.
…It was probably creepy from the outside.
Rena noticed my strange smile and peeked at my face with a worried look.

"What's wrong, Kei-kun?
Did you hit your head? …Did you?"

"My ukemi is impeccable.
If you want, I can do it again?"
changeReason: New translation uses the specific martial arts term 'ukemi' (breakfall) from the JPN 受身, replacing the vague 'graceful landing.'

TextENGNew: "N-no thanks, really…!"
What's with that — what exactly do you mean, "no thanks"?
changeReason: Old renders the indignant そんなのとはなんだ as a neutral 'That would be what...?' while JPN conveys indignation at being dismissed.

TextENGNew:
"Alright, Sensei's here~!
Everyone take your seats~!"
At Mion's call, everyone shuffled into their chairs.

"Good morning, everyone.
You had your festival yesterday.
Thank you to everyone who stayed to help with the cleanup."
"Now, for the next few days, TV and magazine reporters who came to cover the festival may still be around."
I had been letting the announcement wash over me as usual, but then the tone in Sensei's voice shifted slightly, and my attention snapped back.

"Some of you may be approached for an interview. Please be careful not to say anything vague or careless.
…Do you understand?"
changeReason: Original renders 曖昧なことやいい加減なこと as 'silly or something taken the wrong way'; new correctly translates as 'vague or careless,' matching the teacher's specific warnings about media interviews.

TextENGNew: Yes~! everyone chorused.
She chose her words carefully, but I knew exactly what she meant.
changeReason: Original misinterprets 言葉を濁していた as 'words were jovial'; new correctly translates as 'chose her words carefully' (being deliberately vague/evasive).

TextENGNew: After all — this was the fifth year.
The fifth-year curse — reporters from tabloid TV shows and second-rate magazines looking for a story would probably be wandering around half-curious.
As far as the village was concerned, it only made their reputation worse, and was thoroughly unwelcome.
She was telling us not to stoke or feed that appetite ourselves.
changeReason: Original loses the subject ('not going to give them what they want') while new correctly identifies the teacher as telling students not to stoke reporters' appetite, per JPN こちらから盛り上げたり提供するな.

TextENGNew: Now I was one of the people on the inside.

And so, understood, I agreed with it.
Fitting yourself to the village's way of doing small things like this — that was the important part of building bonds with the people here.
I had assumed club activities would be cancelled today, given how tired everyone must be from last night.
Maybe I'd head to the game center in Okinomiya for the first time in a while…
I was in the middle of thinking that when Mion clapped me on the shoulder.

"…What are you spacing out for, Kei-chan?
We're about to start club activities!"
Oh-ho~?! We're doing it!!
Just like that, the plan I'd just been formulating vanished into thin air.
No sign of last night's exhaustion anywhere — that's Mion for you!
Not just Mion, either. Rika-chan must have had her share of cleanup duty too.
Satoko, as Rika-chan's tag-along, had been at the shrine until late into the night.
Even so, they all seemed energetic enough — which was impressive — but the invisible fatigue must still be there.

This was too good a chance to pass up.
I was going to grind them into the ground.

"Keiichi.
What are you grinning about?"

"Ohohohoho!
I'm sure you think, since everyone is short on sleep, today is your chance to win!"
Gulp… right on the nose.

"Oh, Keiichi.
Your expression just changed."

"Oh~, Keiichi-san?
Looks like that hit home — ohohohohoho!"

"Hau~, Kei-kun?
That hit home, did it? …Did it?"

"Keh-keh-keh!
Kei-chan, this club is not so weak or so easy as all that!"

All four pairs of eyes gleamed at once.
Indeed — these were battle-hardened veterans; they were not to be underestimated…

"Alright, here's today's game!
Today we'll be playing…!"
Mion slammed a deck of cards down on the desk with a thump.
"A proper mystery game!"

A murmur of interest went around.
An imported game, huh… this sounds interesting!
"The rulebook is in English, but the rules themselves aren't so complicated."
"This game is no weakness of mine, I assure you!"
changeReason: Original says 'I'm no good at this game!' which is the OPPOSITE of the JPN 苦手ではございませんことよ (this game is NOT my weakness); new correctly renders the double-negative as confident.

TextENGNew: "…I won't lose."
Rena's color was looking rather poor. She was apparently not great at this kind of game.
"The rules are: whoever identifies the culprit, the murder weapon, and the crime scene wins!"
There were apparently three categories of cards: culprit, weapon, and crime scene.
Looking at the culprit cards… there was "Mion," "Rika"... everyone's names were on them. Oh — there was already one with my name too!

Hmm.
There were all sorts of weapon cards!
Axe, knife, poison…!

And the crime scene cards were pretty evocative!
The study, the courtyard, the attic… there was even one for "behind the gym!"

"So essentially:
one card from each category is removed from the deck.
Those are the correct answers."

"The remaining cards are shuffled and distributed equally among us.
We take turns asking each other about our cards, trying to deduce which ones were removed."
Ah, I see. Whoever holds no card of a given kind — that's the true culprit!
changeReason: New translation restructures the deduction logic differently from the original, changing how the card game mechanic is understood.

TextENGNew:
"When you think you've figured out the answer, raise your hand!
The game ends and we check if you're right."

"Correct answer scores plus one point!
Wrong answer scores minus one point!
Whether right or wrong the game ends and we reshuffle and start over!
Do you follow, Keiichi-san?"

"It'll make sense once you play.
This game requires a great deal of thought."

"You'll lose track without taking notes~?
Think you can keep up~? Keh-keh-keh!"
Rena hurriedly produced a notepad. The tension on her face was palpable.
"…So what's the penalty game this time, Mion?"
Everyone's eyes focused on Mion at once!

"Well… 
Considering some of us are a bit tired from yesterday, today I'll go easy on you: the Errand Sentence. The loser gets a shopping list from everyone and has to run to the store!"
"So the loser has to go out and buy juice and snacks and stuff."
"I feel today's penalty is somehow too lenient."
…Was it though?
"Keiichi does not seem to think so."
Because you never know what she'll make you buy…
With Mion in charge, if you lose, she'd probably put 'hemorrhoid cream' and 'athlete's foot medicine' on your shopping list — a nightmare of bizarre and humiliating purchases!
I looked at Mion's face. Her eyes seemed to gleam.
Last place was absolutely not an option.
"Alright, let's begin!"
The cards were shuffled at once and dealt out.
Since one card from each category had been removed, every card in your hand was by definition "innocent."
"First up is the person who worked hardest last night — Rika-chan!"
changeReason: Original mistranslates 一番がんばった as 'came in first place'; it actually means 'worked the hardest' (referring to festival cleanup), which the new translation corrects.

TextENGNew:
"Thank you.
Mii~ then — 'Satoko,' 'Axe,' 'Courtyard'!"
You choose one each from culprit, weapon, and crime scene, then ask everyone.
If anyone holds at least one of the named cards, they must answer "I have it." (The point being that you don't have to say which one.)
"Ah… I've got one."
"Rena too."
"…Me too."
"Oh my, everyone has one, then."

Three people said they held a 'Satoko,' 'Axe,' or 'Courtyard' card.
So all three of those were cleared of suspicion. Note, note…
changeReason: New translation adds extra deductive commentary ('So all three were cleared of suspicion. Note, note…') that belongs to the next entry, merging two separate lines.

TextENGNew: So all three of them — 'Satoko,' 'Axe,' 'Courtyard' — were in the clear. Note, note…

"In that case, I shall try:
'Keiichi,' 'Knife,' 'Attic'!"
Hmm, only two people said they had one.
Which means one of the three — 'Keiichi,' 'Knife,' or 'Attic' — might be the culprit, weapon, or crime scene!
Wait, though — 'having' doesn't say how many cards you hold!
It's possible that one of the two who answered — Rena or Mion — holds two of the three…!!
This was… a psychological game as well.
"Alright, my turn~!"
Wa— wa-wait — I'm still catching up!

"Too slow, Keiichi-san!
I'm afraid we've left you in the dust!"
"Ugh, stop! Just hold on!"

…This game was more demanding than I'd expected.
Inside, I was starting to feel the strain… a premonition of struggle!!
"Everyone ready?"
"…'Rena,' 'Pickaxe,' 'Riverbank'!"
Only one person said they had this one… which meant…
Wait, wait — stay calm and think —
"Oh yeah!! Culprit confirmed!!"
As we fumbled along through the game, Mion suddenly raised her hand.
"The culprit is 'Rika-chan'!"

"The weapon is the 'Dumbbell,' crime scene is 'Behind the Gym'!
Well?!"
We drew the answer cards from the box to check against Mion's deduction…
"Correct!!"

"Fuwaaah~!
I was so close~~!"
"I couldn't decide between 'dumbbell,' 'poison,' or 'pistol' — I just couldn't narrow it down!!"

"Dumbbells are heavy.
I prefer poison — nice and slow."

Th-that's not the point here??
Such a sweet face… still totally ruthless…

"……Keiichi.
Please don't look at me with frightened eyes.
Mii~"
Huh? …Was my face that obvious?

"Ohohohohoho!
Keiichi-san's face is always so easy to read!"

…Even so, this game was harder than I had imagined.
Inside, I was starting to feel real pressure.

…And now that I looked, Rena wasn't looking so great either.
It seemed like she and I were at a decided disadvantage in this game.

Our eyes met.
She was thinking the same thing.

"My my, those two~?
You look like you're in trouble!
Going to tie for last together~? Keh-keh-keh!"

Absolutely not!!
A pitiful man sent to buy 'hemorrhoid cream' with girls at his side… the very thought made me shudder!
"…Keiichi looks like he has trouble with this game."
Wh— what?!

"Th-that is absolutely not true!!
Next round, I'll show you the true abilities of the great detective Maebara!"

"Oh, you said it!!
Then I shall demolish you without mercy~!"
"Satoko… want another taste?"
changeReason: Original 'I'll make you eat those words' differs from JPN また喰らいたいかぁ which means 'want to get hit again?' (referring to forehead flicks); new captures the threatening tone better.

TextENGNew: I gave two or three threatening flicks of my index finger toward her — ping! ping!

"Fuwaaah!!
Keiichi-san is threatening me~~!!"

"There there.
…Keiichi, resorting to force because you're not confident is unfair."
"…Yes ma'am."
When Rika-chan said it, I had no choice but to reconsider.
I reluctantly holstered my forehead-flicking weapon.
After a few more rounds, I started to get the hang of it — but no matter what I tried, I'd always fall one step behind at the crucial moment.
…Mion was apparently reading people's expressions as well as their questions. Just strategy alone wasn't enough to beat Mion, the self-declared 'master.'
To overturn this overwhelming disadvantage, I would have to commit to ruthlessness…!!
Forget deduction… switch to a winning-at-all-costs club mindset.
The important thing wasn't to find the culprit — it was to win!!
…Ah-ha!
This was the play… the only play!!!

"Oh, sorry.
Just going to the bathroom."
I made my bet.
Clear my head… get some fresh air, let the mind reset.

Two, three times — breathe in~ breathe out~ — deep breaths.
For good measure, I extended my arms out front and swung them in wide pendulum arcs, bending deep at the knees for some good squats.
One, two, three… focused entirely on loosening up my muscles.
changeReason: Original adds 'still on edge' which contradicts the JPN 余念がなかった (was absorbed in/focused entirely on); new correctly says 'focused entirely on loosening up.'

TextENGNew: When I glanced out, I saw classmates who had also stayed after school playing noisily in the schoolyard.
Back in Tokyo I never spent afternoons like this, playing around after school.
Looking back on it now, why did my life back then feel, somehow, abnormal?
My gaze, without my directing it, drifted off into the distance.
changeReason: Original misreads 自然 as 'natural scenery' but here it means 'naturally/involuntarily'; new correctly translates as 'my gaze, without my directing it, drifted off.'

TextENGNew: I snapped back. I couldn't keep this up.

There was a penalty game on the line.
Now: would this plan work?
The success of it would hinge entirely on teamwork.
Just then, Rena came out of the classroom, tilting her head curiously.
"…Kei-kun, did you call for me?"
When I had stood up, I had caught Rena's eye on the way out.

It had gotten through to her.
We really might be well-suited to each other.
With this…
There was no time, so I asked Rena directly how many points she had at this point.
Rena hesitated a moment, then opened her mouth tentatively.

"Um… let's see… points… minus six.
Why are you asking?"
I had guessed from her manner, but I hadn't expected quite this bad…
"At this rate, one of us is going to end up last."
"Ha-hau~, …so that means Rena's getting the penalty game? …Does it?"

"Hey, don't give up so easily!
Let's go for the win, alright?! Let's win!"
"H-how?!"

I leaned in close to Rena's ear.
Whisperwhisperwhisper…

"Ehhh~?
I-is that… allowed? Is it?"

"Remember, Rena.
Club rule number two — spare no effort in the pursuit of victory!!"

"Y-yeah… you're right!
I'll — I'll do my best~!"
Rena struck a full-body fighting pose.
Our strategy required teamwork above all else to succeed.
When the game started and everyone was dealt their cards, there was always a brief window where everyone's eyes went to their hand to start taking notes.
That was the moment — Rena, who sat next to me, and I would flash our cards to each other under the desk in an instant.

Do it under the table, quick.
Keep your pencil moving; never stop writing.

Even if the others looked up, what draws the eye first is movement — that's just human nature.
…That was the key.
If it worked, we'd go undiscovered for quite a while!
Having twice as much information as the others meant no weaknesses in our questioning either!!

"Hau~, you're right!
This way we might actually win today!"

"Might win — no.
We will win!"
And then we'd send Mion out to buy 'hemorrhoid cream' and 'athlete's foot medicine'! Mufufufufu…
"Kei-kun, your face is… a little scary…"
We're going to win, Rena. A face or two can look however they want.
"R-right… that's also true!"
The two of us shared a grin.
By the time Mion caught on, Rena and I would have gone past avoiding last place — we'd have locked up first and second…!
This was looking good!! Watch out, Mion!!!
After Rena went back to the classroom, I waited a beat, then followed.
I tried not to meet Mion's eyes.
Because as they say, the eyes say as much as the mouth.
Mion had good instincts. If she caught on, our whole scheme would be for nothing.
changeReason: Original truncates the sentence to just 'Mion is pretty smart'; new includes the full meaning about her good instincts and the risk of their scheme being exposed per the complete JPN.

TextENGNew: I took the utmost care.
changeReason: Original 'I carefully looked her over' misinterprets 細心の注意を払った which means 'I exercised the utmost caution'; new correctly translates the intent.

TextENGNew: Unaware of my conspiracy, Mion declared with great confidence:
changeReason: Original 'I was fairly confident that Mion had no knowledge' restructures the sentence losing the JPN subject (Mion declared with confidence); new correctly has Mion declaring while unaware.

TextENGNew: "Alright, let's resume the game!"
Mion thrust her arm in the air — and just then, Sensei Chie came in.

"Sonozaki-san.
Might I have a moment?"
"What is it, Sensei?"
"Sorry everyone, just a pause."
With that she stood, and went to the teacher.

"You have a visitor, Sonozaki-san.
Please go to the entrance hall."

"A visitor?
For me?"

"That's right.
Go quickly, please."
"Understood."

"…Anyway.
I'll be back in a bit, everyone."
changeReason: Original has Mion calling herself 'This ol' man' (her おじさん self-reference); new loses this characterization by saying 'I'll be back in a bit, everyone.'

TextENGNew: With that, Mion slipped briskly out of the classroom.
"Who do you think is visiting Mi-chan? …Do you?"
"Mi-san knows a great many people — someone visits her every now and then."
"Yes, Mii knows a lot of people."
"…I guess it can't be helped."
I was watching the direction Mion had gone with a resentful look.
What a pain — I was planning to make her cry uncle!
…Still, a visitor for Mion — who could it be?

We waited for a while.
But there was no sign of Mion coming back.
"…Mion-san is taking quite a while to return."
changeReason: Original says 'I'm sure Mion-san wouldn't go home without us' (reassurance); new correctly translates as 'Mion-san is taking quite a while to return' (observation of delay), matching JPN なかなか帰っていらっしゃらない.

TextENGNew:
"We have nothing to do, like piggies with idle hooves.
Oink oink~"
"Pi-piggies with idle hooves…? Hau~♪"

"Haaau~ little piggie Rika-chan is so cuuute~!
Can I take you hooome~?☆"
"We're still in the middle of a game, you know."

"…Hauuu~?!
But but, she's so cute~! Hau~♪"
"No matter how cute, no until the game is over."
"But just a little bit… is that not allowed? …Is it?"
"After it's over."
Hmm. This was a problem.
Everyone was getting restless — idle-hooved piggies, that is — unable to sit still.
"Can't be helped, let's restart the game!"
At that, everyone's eyes lit up and they nodded.

Right… if you think about it, this is the perfect 'while the cat's away'…
In a sense, the 'chance' had arrived.
And so, with Mion's seat left empty, we shuffled the cards and dealt them out.
Rika-chan and Satoko, predictably, put their heads down and began scribbling notes.
Rena and I exchanged a glance and, under the pretense of taking notes, showed each other our cards under the desk.
'Satoko'… 'Rena'… 'Pistol'… 'Cleaver'… 'Cemetery'… 'Storage Shed'…
changeReason: Card name changed from 'Knife' to 'Cleaver' for 包丁; these are different weapon types in the deduction game context.

TextENGNew: …Which meant all the other cards were likely to be under suspicion.
"Mufufu… mufufufu…"
Rena hurriedly jabbed her elbow into my ribs.
"What are you laughing about, Keiichi-san?"

"…That is deeply unsettling.
Mii~"
…No good, no good — this was not the moment to laugh. I needed a plausible cover story…
"Oh… I was just imagining this group actually committing crimes! Keh-keh-keh!"
"…I don't understand the logic, but somehow I find it deeply unpleasant."

"Satoko, don't engage.
Keiichi is almost certainly trying to lead you astray."

Satoko and Rika-chan exchanged a glance and giggled — "ohohoh" / "nipaa~"
Rena was sweating beside them.

"Then it shall be my turn, as I am currently in second place.
I'll try… 'Rena,' 'Pistol,' 'Attic'!"
"Oh, I've got one."
"Rena too."
"I have one as well."
"Is that so — everyone has one."
…Which means the location… and the weapon… is at risk.
Let me try a feint here.

"My turn now.
Okay then — 'Rika,' 'Ashtray,' 'Lounge'!"
"I have one."
"I do too."
"…Rena doesn't."
So that means… not those, but maybe… and if so…
As I was putting Xs in my notes, something clicked.
"Yesss, culprit confirmed!!"

"Wha?!
Already?!"
"Keiichi, men who are too hasty are disliked…"
I thrust my index finger straight up at the ceiling and proclaimed the culprit, weapon, and crime scene in full.

"Culprit: 'Mion'!
Weapon: 'Axe'! Crime scene: 'Library'!
…Well?!!"
We drew the answer cards from the box to check my deduction…
"Kei-kun, correct!!"
"Ungh — and to think it was Mion-san!"

"An axe is no good.
Poison — nice and slow — that's the ideal."

Rika-chan, that's just your personal preference.
Still — a soft face hiding something hard, through and through…
On that note, Rena and I kept up our winning streak — but Rika-chan and Satoko were no fools.
Not long after our streak began, they started glancing back and forth between us, with precisely timed attention to our movements.
changeReason: Original says 'we had to start being more cautious'; new correctly describes Rika and Satoko starting to watch them closely, matching the JPN about opponents observing their behavior.

TextENGNew: And then, finally…
The surprise was — what I'd taken for note-taking: Satoko had a small hand mirror under the desk.
Through the mirror, Rika-chan's sharp gaze was staring intently — at me.
I scrambled to cover, but it was already too late.
"Oh my… so that's how it was, is it… both of you."
"Mii — if that's the case, we have our own plan."
Rika-chan and Satoko looked at each other and both broke into grins.
From that point on, the game became a head-to-head between Team Rena-and-Me versus Team Satoko-and-Rika-chan, a back-and-forth battle with neither side gaining the upper hand.
And after about an hour —
It was in the middle of our thirteenth round that Mion finally came back.
"…What happened, Mi-chan?"
Rena noticed something and asked.
Mion did seem off, now that I thought about it. She seemed somehow less energetic.
"…Hm? No, I'm fine."
She said that, but the look on her face was dispirited enough that you'd want to ask if she was really all right.
…But the next moment —
"Whew. …Wait — wha, whaaaaaaaaat!"
"What IS thiiiis~~!!"
And no wonder.
While Mion was away, the four of us had racked up a staggering number of points, leaving her far behind.
The instant she looked at the score sheet, Mion understood: no matter how hard she tried from here, she couldn't overturn anyone's lead.
"Nooo~, I was going to send someone out to do my shopping today~~"
Just as Mion slumped in dejection, a slip of paper fluttered out of her pocket.
I picked it up and looked… WHAT??!!
"Wow… there's a lot written on it."

"…Two cakes of tofu.
Shampoo and conditioner.
Mirin and fried tofu…
What is this?"
You called it the 'Errand Sentence'…
"W-wait, this is the shopping list people already asked YOU to do!!"
changeReason: Original 'a ploy to make us run errands' differs from new 'this is the shopping list people already asked YOU to do'; the JPN reveals Mion's own errands were being offloaded, not a generic ploy.

TextENGNew: Mion gave an oily little laugh.
"…You started this whole game just to get people to run your errands for you…"
But seeing Mion back to her usual self, I felt a wave of relief.
Although — it did seem a little sad that the penalty game had been decided while she was away…
But then —

"Here you go, Mi-chan.
These are Rena's errands, please and thank you☆"
…Wait, you were writing a shopping list next to me while I was worried about her?!
"Goodness~, we had ours ready too~~!"

"…How unfortunate.
Mii~"
Even though it was a close thing… when did you even write those lists??!!

I felt cold sweat trickling down my spine.
These people are demons…
